findings.title: Verification guard removed from payment capture
findings.evidence: The diff removes the `if (isVerified(order))` check that previously gated `chargeCard(order)`. After this change, `chargeCard` is called unconditionally for every order regardless of verification status, allowing unverified or fraudulent orders to be charged. The Diff Drift report flags this explicitly: '`chargeCard(…)` ran behind a guard before this change and now runs unconditionally — confirm the check wasn't load-bearing.'

notes: Only one file changed in this diff (`src/capture.ts`). The raw diff confirms the report snippet exactly — no discrepancy between report and patch.

The report severity is 'medium'; this reviewer accepts that rating as-is rather than escalating, since no additional context (e.g. whether `isVerified` is called upstream) is available in the packet. However, the risk of charging unverified orders is severe enough in most payment systems that the change warrants a block until the intent is confirmed.
No package.json or lockfile changes are present, so dependency risk is not a factor here.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 multi-model panel; blind, packet-only context.
